items.text: Smooth broadband synth whoosh with fast attack and clean mid-range swoop, wide stereo spread, slight downward pitch bend, used as a clean radio transition between segments, under 2 seconds.

Orchestral marcato string chop: descending two-note stab from cello and viola section, dry attack with tight hall resonance, falling pitch gesture with immediate cutoff, in D minor, 100 BPM, used as a decisive closing transition to end a segment.

Rising granular synth transition: digital grain cloud building from noise floor to dense shimmering texture, mid-range cluster with spectral smear, wide stereo with slight upward pitch rise, used as a contemporary radio transition for arts or experimental segments.

Cinematic tape-stop transition: rapid deceleration effect like a tape machine powering down, pitch dropping from normal speed to slow warble, mid-frequency warmth with slight wow and flutter, tight tail under 1 second, used as a retro dramatic transition for vintage-flavored segments.

FM radio tuning sweep: rapid scan across radio frequencies with bursted static fragments, sudden clear tone flashing through the noise, mid-to-high pitch sweep, tight dry envelope, used as a radio-authentic transition for communications or broadcast-themed segments.

Reverse cymbal swell: shimmering wash building from silence to crash peak, wide stereo field with deep reverb tail, high-mid metallic texture, ending abruptly at the crest, used as a dreamy cross-fade transition.

Electric guitar slide transition: smooth fret slide from fifth fret to twelfth fret on wound strings, warm mid-range tone with live room ambience, single clean sliding gesture, used as a relaxed music-segment transition.

Classic vinyl record scratch: abrupt stop with textured groove grain, close warm microphone perspective, subtle needle surface noise, short mid-low-frequency abrasion, used as a nostalgic retro transition cue.

Flowing wind blow transition: airy full-frequency swoosh with wide open-field spatial movement, subtle stereo pan from left to right, used as a natural ambient transition for nature or documentary programming.

Mechanical gear shift lever clunking into place: metallic impact with dry staccato resonance, short mid-low metallic ping, close perspective, used as an industrial transition for automotive or engineering segments.

Sudden orchestral string sforzando accent: sharp dissonant attack from full string section, mid-high bite with concert hall decay tail, one-shot dramatic punch with fast decay, used as a tension transition for drama cues.

Gated sub-bass countdown: three staccato low-frequency hits with increasing tempo, resonant filter closing between each strike, mono-to-narrow spatial treatment, deadened silence at the end, under 2 seconds, used as a dramatic countdown transition for show segments.

Rolling tribal drum crescendo: accelerating skin hits on djembe and conga, percussive mid-frequency build culminating in a final slamming accent, natural live stage ambience, used as an energetic transition for world music or sports segments.

Fast piano glissando sweeping upward from middle C to high C, rippling bright mid-high tones with natural room resonance, single fluid hand gesture across the keyboard, used as an elegant classical transition.

Resonant filter sweep on sustained synth pad: cutoff opening from closed muted tone to bright resonant peak, mid-band frequency emphasis with stereo auto-pan movement, used as a filtered transition building anticipation for a reveal.

Wordless vocal choir swell: building from soft breathy murmur to full harmonic peak, airy cathedral-wide spatial bloom, mid-low to high frequency expansion, used as an uplifting inspirational transition for reflective segments.

Abrupt white noise burst: sizzling full-band texture with immediate attack and instant cutoff, no tail or sustain, dry presentation, under 1 second, used as a clean signal-shift transition between radio segments.

Falling brass horn bend: single descending jazz-influenced note from F to C in the mid-low register, warm round tone with hall reverb tail, used as a sophisticated late-night segment transition, 80 BPM feel.

Rhythmic gated synth stutter: chopped granular pattern of repeated mid-high bursts with gated amplitude modulation, tight compressed delivery, used as a modern electro transition for contemporary radio shows.

Massive hydraulic press impact: heavy mechanical thud with compressed air release, low-mid metallic resonance ring, close industrial spatial perspective, tight deadened cutoff, used as an industrial transition for engineering or heavy-content programming shifts.
